data.title: The S&P 500 election-year implied return from now until year-end reaches 6,200 – analyst
data.description:
The median S&P 500 (SP500), (SPX) return from Oct. 15 to Dec. 31 during election years has been 7.04% since 1928.

But applying the S&P 500’s (SP500) 5,860 level, achieved on Monday, “implies a year-end level of 6,270," wrote finance writer Mike Zaccardi on X.com.

Credits to Mike Zaccardi, X.com

The index is currently at 5,821, up 0.12% from yesterday, up 3.41% from a month ago, and up 22.05% year-to-date.

Top stocks from the S&P 500 (SP500) today:

  • United Airlines Holdings (UAL) – Up 8.79%
  • Morgan Stanley (MS) – Up 6.8%
  • UB Bancorp (USB) – Up 5.4%
  • J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) – Up 5.1%
  • Universal Health Services (UHS) – Up 4.9%
  • Warner Bros Discovery (WBD) – Up 4.4%
  • Delta Air Lines (DAL) – Up 4.1%
  • Micron Technology (MU) – Up 3.4%
  • HCA Holdings (HCA) – Up 3.3%
  • Dexcom (DXCM) – Up 2.9%

data.title: Corteva, Westlake upgraded at Bank of America
data.description:
Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) and Westlake (NYSE:WLK) on Wednesday were upgraded by analysts at Bank of America as they re-rated makers of specialty chemicals and fertilizers.

Corteva upgrade

Corteva (CTVA) was upgraded to Buy from a previous investment rating of Neutral by Bank of America.

The bank said Corteva (CTVA) is poised to expand its share of the agricultural chemicals market with its Enlist brand of weed controllers.

“We rate Corteva (CTVA) Buy given several tailwinds that position the company for success in 2025 and beyond,” Steve Byrne, analyst at Bank of America, said in an October 16 report. “These include higher corn seed prices, continued Enlist seed market share gains, especially if branded Dicamba labels are not renewed, likely duties on 2'4-D imports, significant ‘controllable’ cost tailwinds, and benefits from gene editing.”

The bank raised its price target on Corteva (CTVA) to $67 a share from $59 a share previously, by rolling over its forecast from 2024 to 2025, and lowering its estimated enterprise value-to-earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization multiple to 11.5 times from 12.5 times previously.

Westlake upgrade

Westlake (WLK) was upgraded to Neutral from a previous investment rating of Underperform by Bank of America.

“Near term, we remain cautious on polyvinyl chloride supply and demand as it appears new U.S. capacity is hitting the market while housing demand is sluggish,” Byrne said in the report. “We expect another 3cpp price erosion through year-end. We also believe that Housing and Infrastructure Projects earnings should remain flattish next year as U.S. housing fundamentals stay challenged and price/cost benefits dwindle.”

Bank of America foresees longer-term demand growth for polyvinyl chloride, which is the third-most produced synthetic polymer of plastic. More factory capacity for the polymer isn’t likely to be added until the end of the decade, according to Bank of America.

The bank raised its price target on Westlake (WLK) to $159 a share from $151 a share previously, rolling forward its forecast to 2025 from 2024, and lowering the estimated enterprise value-to-earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization multiple to 8 times from 8.5 times previously.

Eastman Chemical, CF Industries downgraded

Eastman Chemical (EMN) was downgraded to Neutral from Buy, and CF Industries Holdings (CF) was lowered to Underperform from Neutral by Bank of America.

data.title: Target is expected to get a boost from Taylor Swift merchandise in the holiday quarter
data.description:
Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) announced this week that two exclusive Taylor Swift releases available will be available only at the retailer starting on Black Friday.

The Minneapolis-based company said the official "Taylor Swift | The Eras Tour Book" provides a behind-the-scenes look at the making and performance of Taylor Swift | The Eras Tour, including never-before-seen photos and personal reflections written by Taylor. "The Tortured Poets Department: The Anthology" album on vinyl and CD has 35 tracks, including four acoustic bonus songs. The items will be available exclusively at Target in stores only on Black Friday, before becoming available on the Target app and on Target.com starting Saturday, November 30.

UBS thinks the Taylor Swift merchandise will provide a modest lift to Q4 sales. "In light of what's anticipated to be a volatile holiday season, we think these launches should provide a differentiated driver of traffic. This is important, given that skeptics believe TGT could see negative comps in 4Q, largely due to 5 fewer shopping days this holiday season," updated analyst Michael Lasser. He added that while that concern is quite understandable, UBS thinks TGT's exclusive merchandise, improved innovation, recent loyalty initiatives, and easy general merchandise comparisons should provide support for a solid Q4 earnings report.

The holiday quarter for the retail sector is the most important of the year and typically leads to the biggest share price swings after sales updates.

data.title: Barrick posts lower than expected Q3 gold output but maintains full-year guidance
data.description:
Barrick Gold (NYSE:GOLD) -0.9% in early trading Wednesday after reporting preliminary Q3 production results that showed lower gold output and gains in copper, while maintaining full-year production guidance.

Q3 total preliminary gold output fell less than 1% from the previous quarter to 943K oz, below the 975K oz analyst consensus estimate from data compiled by LSEG, while copper production increased 11% Q/Q to 48K metric tons of copper.

Q3 operational highlights included a 23% Q/Q production increase at the Pueblo Viejo mine in the Dominican Republic due to continued plant optimization and a stronger quarter at North Mara in Tanzania driven by higher grades.

Barrick (GOLD) said Q3 copper production was driven primarily by higher grades and recoveries at Lumwana in Zambia.

The company said it continues to expect a materially stronger Q4 and deliver full-year production in line with earlier guidance of 3.9M-4.3M oz of gold and 180K-210K metric tons of copper.

data.title: Nokia in discussions with Airtel for multi-billion dollar 5G contract - report
data.description:
Nokia (NYSE:NOK) is in discussions with Bharti Airtel about securing a multi-billion dollar agreement to provide 5G telecom equipment to the Indian mobile operator, which is bolstering its network, Reuters reported citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The deal would be for Nokia's latest AirScale mobile radios, which support upgrading an existing network to 5G-Advanced and cuts energy costs, the report added.

Sweden's Ericsson (ERIC) has already won a multi-billion dollar contract from Bharti Airtel, according to a previous report.

India is the world's second-largest smartphone market and companies like Airtel, Reliance's Jio and Vodafone Idea (VOD) have been investing billions of dollars to upgrade their networks to 5G, the report noted.

Finland-based Nokia is slated to report its third-quarter results on Thursday.

Nokia and Airtel did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for a comment.

data.title: Wave stock soars 66% on positive data for RNA editing treatment (update)
data.description:
Wave Life Sciences (NASDAQ:WVE) shares soared 66% Wednesday morning after the company announced positive proof-of-mechanism data from its Phase 1b/2a RestoraAATion-2 study for its RNA editing treatment candidate WVE-006 for alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, or AATD.

WVE-006 was developed using Wave’s oligonucleotide chemistry platform and is designed to address AATD-related lung disease and/or liver disease. Wave said the proof-of-mechanism data are “the first-ever clinical demonstration of RNA editing in humans,” according to a statement.

The company expects to release multi-dose data from the study next year.

U.K. drugmaker GSK (NYSE:GSK) holds the exclusive global license for WVE-006 and will assume development and commercialization responsibilities for the product after Wave wraps up the RestorAATion-2 study. Wave said it is eligible to receive up to $525M in milestone payments plus tiered royalties on net sales for WVE-006 under the GSK agreement.

Wave added that it plans to highlight the WVE-006 data along with data from its GalNAc-RNA editing programs and preclinical data for its obesity drug candidate WVE-007 during its Annual Research Day on Oct. 30.

Late Tuesday, Wave said in an SEC filing that Takeda (TAK) has decided not to exercise its option on a clinical candidate for Huntington's disease, WVE-003. Wave plans to continue development on the product.

(Updates with recent stock movement, Takeda announcement.)

data.title: Main Street Capital invests $41M in distributor serving Canada telecom industry
data.description:
• Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) completed a $41.4M investment in Connect Telecommunications Solutions, a value-added distributor of fiber management to the Canadian telecom industry, adding to its portfolio.
  • The investment facilitates the minority recapitalization of Connect Telecommunications. Main Street (MAIN) partnered with the company's founders to execute the deal.
  • Main Street's investment included a combination of first-lien, senior secured term debt and direct equity investment.
  • Main Street (MAIN) stock rose 1.2% in Wednesday morning trading.

data.title: IBM reaches record high, receives Buy rating ahead of earnings report: Stifel
data.description:
IBM (NYSE:IBM) received a Buy rating from Stifel ahead of its upcoming third quarter financial results as Big Blue shares closed at a record high earlier this week.

Stifel also increased it price target on IBM shares to $246 from $205. IBM closed on Monday with a record high of $235.26.

Although Stifel expects IBM to report lower revenue than the quarter prior due to seasonality, earnings per share should likely beat estimates.

IBM is slated to report third quarter 2024 financial results on Oct. 23 after markets close. A consensus of analysts expects adjusted earnings per share of $2.22 on revenue of $15.06B. Last quarter, IBM beat the EPS estimate by $0.26 and revenue by $147M.

Looking ahead to 2025, Stifel believes recent acquisitions by IBM along with a new mainframe cycle, will allow IBM to grow revenue.

IBM is expected to close on its $6.4B acquisition of HashiCorp (HCP) during the fourth quarter of 2024, but regulatory reviews could push that into early 2025. IBM closed on its $2.1B acquisition of StreamSets and webMethods from Software AG on July 1.

Stifel expects these acquisitions to add 4% or more to IBM's software segment revenue in 2025. The investment firm also believes IBM will benefit from enterprise license agreement renewals in 2025, particularly in the fourth quarter. What's more, IBM's next mainframe cycle is expected to start during the first half of 2025.

"IBM's last mainframe cycle launched in 2Q22 with z16," said Stifel analysts, led by David Grossman, in an investor note. "Mainframe cycles typically last ~10-12 quarters, and we expect the next cycle to begin in 1H25."

"Segment reporting is not that detailed," he added. "However, historically, mainframe cycles add ~$1.5B to segment revenue (Infrastructure) in the first 12 months."

IBM shares have soared 67% over the past year, and have reached several record highs over the past month.

data.title: Airline stocks hit a 52-week high after solid results from United Airlines
data.description:
The Dow Jones U.S. Airlines Index (DJUSAR) and the U.S. Global Jets ETF (NYSEARCA:JETS) both hit new highs in early trading on Wednesday after United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) topped expectations with its Q3 earnings report and announced a new $1.5 billion buyback program. The positive read from United (UAL) took some of the sting off Delta Air Lines (DAL) earnings miss last week.

With its Q3 report, United Airlines (UAL) reported total revenue increased by 2.5% to $14.84B, above the consensus estimate of $14.78B. Domestic revenue was down 0.3% while international revenue fell 2.0%. Corporate revenues were up 13% year over year, and in the quarter premium revenues remained resilient and were up 5% year over year. Revenue from Basic Economy was up 20% year over year. For the current quarter, United (UAL) expects an adjusted profit of $2.50 to $3.00 per share, versus the consensus estimate of $2.68 per share.

On Wall Street, Morgan Stanley said the commencement of the UAL buyback marks another milestone in the airline industry as it puts the pandemic in the rearview mirror. Notably, the UAL Q4 guidance was largely in-line with Wall Street expectations, despite drags from the Florida hurricanes and U.S. election drama.

United Airlines (UAL) jumped 9.6% in early trading on Wednesday. Other gainers included Spirit Airlines (SAVE) +4.3%, American Airlines Group (AAL) +3.9%, Delta Air Lines (DAL) +3.6%, JetBlue Airways (JBLU) +2.2%, and Southwest Airlines (LUV) +2.1%.
